channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Flow apuesta por tap to phone para expandir pagos físicos en LatAm, sin desplegar terminales
item.description: La fintech chilena Flow quiere convertir el smartphone en la próxima terminal de cobro para comercios en América Latina y, para lograrlo, trabaja en el despliegue de la tecnología tap to phone en Chile, México y Perú. Con esta apuesta, la empresa busca entrar con más fuerza al mundo presencial sin depender del modelo tradicional de terminales físicas. Accede al análisis que impulsa la toma de decisiones en la banca latinoamericana Este contenido exclusivo está disponible solo para miembros registrados de iupana.

item.title: Viewpoint: Why Speedy Payouts Matter in EU Plan to Close Insurance Protection Gap
item.description: With a lot of recent climate events, insurance protection gaps have appeared, further complicating the financial implications of weather volatility. A recent report from the European Insurance and Occupational Pensions Authority (EIOPA) pointed to growing protection gaps for floods, earthquakes and wildfires, highlighting Greece and Italy as the nations with the highest total protection gaps for natural disasters. Catastrophe insurance data provider PERILS recently increased its initial loss estimate as a result of the storm from €467 million to €479 million ($548.5 million to $562.6 million).

item.title: Cyber Insurance Market Faces Pressure as Claims Severity Climbs
item.description: While the cyber insurance market is seeing more capacity and increased competition, insurers are still battling with the challenge of increasing frequency and severity of claims, according to John Menefee, enterprise cyber lead at Travelers Bond and Specialty Insurance, in a conversation with Carrier Management at Travelers’ New York City office. Everyone is fighting for market share on new business at a time where there’s been an increase in frequency and severity of claims.” “It’s been a soft market for a few years running, and 2025 was a continuation of that,” he said.

item.title: Aon, Zurich, Howden, Sompo: 11 APAC insurance people moves of the week
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Mid-year renewals seen down 15-20%+, cat bonds more of a competitive threat: Dutt, Aeolus
item.description: The mid-year 2026 property catastrophe reinsurance renewals are seeing pricing outcomes skewing towards the worst end of a -15% to-20%+ range, while catastrophe bonds have become more of a competitive threat to reinsurers now given longer-durations of cover and better rates, according to Aditya Dutt, President of Aeolus Capital Management. At the mid-year renewals, property catastrophe reinsurance pricing is seen to be down 15% to 20% compared to the prior year, a faster decline than the 10% to 15% declines seen at January 1st. But Dutt told the analysts that outcomes are skewing more towards the worst end of that range, with pricing pressure most intense in US nationwide accounts and the Florida market cited as the most competitive.

item.title: Allstate starts 2026 annual aggregate risk period with $870m of April catastrophe losses
item.description: US primary insurer Allstate has begun the new annual aggregate risk period, that its catastrophe bonds and excess of loss reinsurance are subject to, with a reported $870 million of pre-tax catastrophe losses in April 2026. The risk period for Allstate’s annual aggregate catastrophe bonds under its Sanders Re program, which sit in the nationwide aggregate tower, begins on April 1st each year. It’s worth highlighting that Allstate said that the $870 million of pre-tax catastrophe losses from April 2026, which translates to $687 million after-tax, were as a result of 10 wind and hail events.

item.title: Three new P&C insurers enter recovering Florida market: OIR
item.description: Three new property and casualty (P&C) insurers have entered Florida’s market as it continues to recover following historic legislative reforms, the Florida Office of Insurance Regulation (OIR) has announced. The additions of Builder Reciprocal Insurance Exchange, Frontline Insurance Reciprocal Exchange and Wingsail Insurance Company brings the total number of insurers entering the Florida market to 20 since the state implemented the reforms. However, following aggressive legislative overhauls aimed at curbing predatory lawsuits and stabilising the market in Florida, insurers are now beginning to return to the state.

item.title: Marsh re-elects full slate of Board Members for one year period
item.description: Marsh, a global firm specialising in insurance broking and risk management, has re-elected its entire slate of 13 Board members as per its discussion during the 2026 Annual Meeting of Stockholders. Stockholders have elected the entire slate of 2026 director nominees for a one-year term expiring at next year’s annual meeting. Last month, Marsh’s global reinsurance broker Guy Carpenter generated revenue of $1.2 billion in the first quarter of 2026, up 3%, or 2% year-on-year on an underlying basis, supporting a robust start to the year for the broking group.

item.title: One Alliance North America enters cat bond market with $115m issuance through One Shield Re
item.description: One Alliance North America Insurance Company, formerly known as Universal North America Insurance Company, has entered the catastrophe bond market through One Shield Re Ltd., its newly established Bermuda-based Special Purpose Insurer. By establishing a long-term platform to access collateralized reinsurance backed by capital markets, One Shield Re aims to diversify One Alliance North America’s reinsurance capacity sources. “It was a privilege to partner with One Alliance North America on their inaugural catastrophe bond,” says Zach Breslin, Head of Lockton Re Capital Markets.

item.title: OSHA Has 6 Inspectors for 60,000 West Virginia Workplaces
item.description: Today, just six federal OSHA inspectors oversee workplace safety for more than 695,000 workers employed across roughly 60,000 workplaces in West Virginia, according to the AFL-CIO’s annual “Death on the Job” report. In fact, the facility had not been inspected again before the fatal incident, highlighting a problem with workplace safety in West Virginia and across the country: federal inspectors do not regularly inspect the most dangerous workplaces. OSHA, or the Occupational Safety and Health Administration, conducts thousands of workplace inspections every year, including hundreds at workplaces in West Virginia.

item.title: These Homeowners Got an Energy-Bill Lifeline. Trump Yanked It Back
item.description: “And they’re not.” “Since day one, President Trump has made American energy dominance a top priority,” EPA Press Secretary Brigit Hirsch said in a statement. The energy demands are a burden on low-income homeowners, who struggle to afford repairs to roofs and windows that would make their homes more airtight and moderate their bills. But after President Donald Trump returned to office in 2025, his Environmental Protection Agency moved to cancel roughly $1.6 billion in previously awarded grants, including the one Holloway was counting on.

item.title: Acrisure to Cut 2,250 Employees, Citing Advances in Technology and AI
item.description: Acrisure did not immediately respond to a request for comment but Williams’ memo said the company is entering a “new phase of execution.” “Advances in technology, [artificial intelligence], and digital platforms are fundamentally changing how businesses operate, how clients expect to be served, and how value is created,” he said. “Leveraging technology must be the core of how we operate, how we grow, and how we delver value to clients every day,” he explained to employees, adding that North America insurance operations will organize “more intentionally around our lines of business.” In October 2025, Acrisure cited advancements in technology and AI when it said it was laying off 400 members of its accounting workforce this year. Acrisure’s CEO told employees that about 2,250 of them won’t be with the company by the end of 2027.

item.title: HR Path acquires Mexico-based TEC360 People
item.description: HR Path , a France-based provider of HR consulting and HRIS solutions, announced the acquisition of TEC360 People, a Mexican company specializing in HR technology and talent solutions. “The acquisition of TEC360 People, a company recognized for its expertise in Cornerstone and SAP Incentive solutions, supports our ambition to become the global reference in HR transformation.” – Frederic Van Bellinghen, Partner at HR Path. “As we continue to focus on the development of our core business within TEC360 Group, joining HR Path ensures continuity for our clients, expanded opportunities for our people, and access to a truly global platform.” – Peter Wiegandt, CEO & Founder of TEC360 Group.

item.title: Allianz expands partnership with supermarket chain Asda
item.description: Allianz UK and Asda are expanding their partnership to launch home and motor insurance products for Asda customers in summer 2026. The partnership builds on a relationship that began more than 15 years ago when Allianz provided Britannia Rescue breakdown coverage to Asda customers. Under the agreement, Asda will market the products while Allianz UK will handle administration, underwriting, and claims management.

item.title: Patriot unveils new brand identity
item.description: Patriot Growth Insurance Services announced a new brand identity as the brokerage continues expanding its national platform and capabilities across property & casualty, employee benefits, personal insurance, risk management, and consulting. Founded in 2019, Patriot said the refreshed branding is designed to reflect its evolution into a more unified national organization while maintaining the local relationships and expertise of its partner agencies. “Patriot has always been built on relationships.

item.title: BBVA Seguros, Ibercaja y Axa aceleran el crecimiento en primas de Vida
item.description: En un contexto en el que el seguro de Vida registró un crecimiento medio del 9,5% en su volumen de primas facturaras en el primer trimestre , varios grupos han superado ampliamente ese umbral, destacando incrementos por encima del doble dígito, según el ranking de Icea. BBVA Seguros avanzó un 23,64% en sus ingresos, consolidando su posición entre los cuatro primeros puestos. Ibercaja fue otro de los protagonistas, con un crecimiento del 23,92%, aunque quizá el dato más llamativo corresponde a Axa , que disparó su facturación un 43,41% en el periodo.

item.title: Helix Marine celebra su 5º aniversario y anuncia su entrada en Portugal
item.description: Helix Marine celebró ayer en Madrid el acto conmemorativo de su 5º aniversario, en el que participaron más de un centenar de profesionales del sector. En su 5º aniversario, Helix Marine quiso también homenajear a varios profesionales del sector, entre los que se encontraba la periodista de INESE, Elena Benito , que fue además quien pronunció las palabras de agradecimiento en nombre de todos ellos. Fue el marco en el que la agencia de suscripción anunció su expansión por la Unión Europea y América Latina (teniendo a Portugal como primer paso, donde ya cuenta incluso con delegado) , y la entrada en nuevas líneas de actividad (como adelantamos hace unos días, ha obtenido autorización para operar en el segmento de Puertos y Terminales), entre ellas Energía , en principio en RC y donde luego ampliará a Ciber, D&O, Daños y Travel.

item.title: Las empresas españolas acusan un déficit en la definición de su propuesta de valor al empleado
item.description: Según el informe, solo el 19% de las empresas en España cuenta con una propuesta de valor al empleado (EVP) definida. Las organizaciones españolas presentan una madurez en el uso de datos superior a la media mundial, pero esa ventaja no se traduce en una gestión más proactiva del capital humano . Así lo concluye el primer ‘Human Capital Trends Study’, publicado por Aon , que analiza el estado de las estrategias de personas en un contexto de aceleración de la inteligencia artificial.

item.title: Lake Tahoe Power Crunch Shows AI’s Growing Energy Toll in West
item.description: Lake Tahoe has long been a refuge for Silicon Valley’s tech elite, from Mark Zuckerberg to Larry Ellison. Energy-hungry data centers across the border in Nevada are adding pressure to the market serving roughly 50,000 electricity customers on the California side of the lake. The squeeze could get worse next year, when Liberty Utilities, the region’s main electric provider, is expected to lose its primary power supplier, Berkshire Hathaway Inc.’s NV Energy.

item.title: Goldman Sachs Paying $500M to Settle Shareholder Suit Over 1MDB Scandal
item.description: Goldman Sachs has agreed to pay $500 million to settle a class action lawsuit accusing the Wall Street bank of defrauding shareholders about its work for 1MDB, a Malaysian sovereign wealth fund that became embroiled in a corruption scandal. Related: Malaysia Enters Mediation With Baer on $112 Million 1MDB Suit Neither Goldman Sachs’ lawyers nor a spokesperson for the bank immediately responded to requests for comment. Goldman helped 1MDB sell $6.5 billion of bonds and collected an estimated $600 million in fees.

item.title: Waymo Suspends Service in Atlanta as Robotaxis Stumped by Floods
item.description: Waymo has temporarily halted service in Atlanta over concerns that its robotaxis may attempt to drive on flooded roads, the latest setback over an issue that recently led the company to recall thousands of vehicles. Related: Waymo Recalls Robotaxis After Vehicle Drove on a Flooded Road The company’s San Antonio service has similarly been on pause since late April, after one of its unoccupied vehicles entered a flooded lane and was swept into a creek. The vehicle in Atlanta encountered the flooded roadway before any National Weather Service alerts about potential flash flooding, which the company monitors to guide its plans when floods occur.

item.title: Anthony Hong joins Howden as head of surety for Asia
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: South Korea to offer US$200m war-risk cover for small shippers in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Gallagher Re confirms Carlos Grijalva as APAC head of cyber
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Allstate secures $200m of Florida reinsurance with Sanders Re III 2026-2 cat bond
item.description: Allstate has now secured its targeted $200 million four year source of Florida catastrophe reinsurance protection with its latest catastrophe bond, pricing the notes from this Sanders Re III Ltd. US primary insurer Allstate returned to the catastrophe bond market earlier this month , aiming to secure a typical May sponsorship of a cat bond to provide it with multi-peril Florida collateralized reinsurance protection. This new Florida-focused cat bond sponsorship takes the number of Sanders Re catastrophe bond deals we’ve seen from Allstate to twenty-five, while it makes twenty-seven cat bonds in total that we’ve tracked and analysed that have been sponsored by the insurer.

item.title: Massachusetts MPIUA returns for $150m Mayflower Re 2026 catastrophe bond
item.description: This new Mayflower Re Series 2026-1 catastrophe bond will become the seventh issuance sponsored by the Massachusetts Property Insurance Underwriting Association (MPIUA), a residual market property insurance association or FAIR Plan for the Commonwealth of Massachusetts. The Massachusetts Property Insurance Underwriting Association (MPIUA) has returned to the catastrophe bond market with an initial target to secure $150 million or more in multi-peril aggregate reinsurance protection through this new Mayflower Re Ltd. For its 2026-1 catastrophe bond sponsorship, the MPIUA is again using its Bermuda-based special purpose insurance vehicle Mayflower Re Ltd.

item.title: AXA XL promotes Goepp to Head of West, Excess Casualty and Bueno to Head of Central, USRM
item.description: French insurer AXA’s property, casualty and specialty risk division, AXA XL, has promoted Nick Goepp to Head of West, Excess Casualty and Lexi Bueno to Head of Central, US Risk Management (USRM). AXA XL explained that Goepp has been a key member of the Excess Casualty West team since 2018, and has experience spanning over a decade in underwriting construction, energy, real estate, and casualty risks. Both Goepp and Bueno will report to Matthew Stitham, Chief Underwriting Officer (CUO), Casualty for the Americas region.

item.title: Weinbach named as CEO-Elect at Radian ahead of Thornberry retirement
item.description: Chief Executive Officer Rick Thornberry has reportedly informed the company’s Board of Directors of his intention to retire in December 2026, with Mike Weinbach set to become CEO-Elect effective June 1, 2026. Since joining Radian as CEO in 2017, Thornberry has more than tripled the company’s book value per share on a total return basis, including dividends, according to the firm, representing a compounded annual growth rate of 13.4%. Thornberry will serve as a strategic advisor through his retirement on December 31, 2026, supporting a disciplined and thoughtful transition.

item.title: Carlos Grijalva joins Gallagher Re as Head of Cyber for APAC
item.description: Gallagher Re, the global reinsurance broking and advisory firm, has announced the appointment of Carlos Grijalva as Executive Director, Head of Cyber for APAC. Gallagher Re said that in his new role, Grijalva will work closely with its APAC regional teams, the global cyber team, and colleagues across Gallagher to develop and expand cyber capabilities across the value chain in the region. Based in Hong Kong, Grijalva reportedly brings extensive experience across underwriting and broking roles in the APAC cyber market.

item.title: Rainy April Delays Corpus Christi’s Projected Water Crisis by 3 Months
item.description: A projection for when Corpus Christi expects to reach a water crisis was pushed back by three months after a wet April brought enough rain to delay an emergency but too little to quench a brutal drought. The city was initially bracing for a Level 1 emergency — the point when water demand is projected to be six months from exceeding supplies — to hit by September. Industrial demand accounts for around 60% of Corpus Christi’s water demand.

item.title: US Finalizes Easement for Dakota Access Crude Oil Pipeline
item.description: The US Army Corps of Engineers finalized an easement for a stretch of the Dakota Access pipeline that passes under a North Dakota lake, ending a years-long environmental review of the hotly contested oil conduit. “We are so pleased with today’s announcement by the Army Corps of Engineers on the issuance of the Record of Decision for the Dakota Access Pipeline’s crossing of Lake Oahe,” Vicki Granado, a spokeswoman for pipeline operator Energy Transfer LP, wrote in an email. “The Dakota Access pipeline has been safely operating for nearly 10 years and is a critical piece of our country’s energy infrastructure.”

item.title: People Moves; Church Mutual Names Kim as CFO, Promotes Schmeltzer to VP of Underwriting, Religious Markets
item.description: Additionally, Church Mutual announced the promotion of Tracy Schmeltzer to vice president of underwriting for religious markets. Schmeltzer has been with Church Mutual for 35 years and most recently served as AVP of Camps, Sports and Outdoor Recreation underwriting. Church Mutual announced several leadership appointments to support its continued growth and operational focus.

item.title: Weathering NOAA’s ‘Below Normal Atlantic Hurricane Season’ And A Super El Niño
item.description: What a super El Niño on one hand and a below-normal Atlantic hurricane season on the other mean for the insurance industry is a matter of perspective: the two climate phenomena are interrelated, fewer hurricanes in one area could be better or worse than more drought and flooding in other places—and the industry is constantly preaching preparedness regardless of the forecast. NOAA’s outlook for the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season, which runs June 1 to November 30, predicts a 35% chance of a near-normal season, a 10% chance of an above-normal season and a 55% chance of a below-normal season. So, we don’t get too caught up in the predictions necessarily as it pertains to what we do with our clients.” On the flip side of NOAA’s Atlantic hurricane season forecast, an increasingly likely super El Niño could by some acounts impact the lives of hundreds of millions of people worldwide.

item.title: U.S. P/C Insurers Post Biggest Q1 Underwriting Profit in 25 Years
item.description: property/casualty insurance industry posted another record in the first quarter of 2026, with a combined ratio of 89.5—before policyholder dividends—standing as the best first-quarter underwriting result in at least 25 years. P/C insurers, at just under 93, was also the best in nearly two decades , surpassed only by a 92.4 ratio recorded 19 years earlier in 2006. Related articles: P/C Statutory Results: The Highs and The Lows ; How State Farm, USAA Boost Customer Retention: Historic Dividends As for the loss ratio component of the combined ratio: Explaining the methodology for their analysis in a research report published on May 20 titled, “Outsized P&C profits persist as Q1 underwriting profit hits new high,” S&P GMI analysts note that the industry-level results for the first quarter of 2026 are an aggregation of individual company results filed with the NAIC available as of May 19.

item.title: ‘We get Asia, we’re committed, we’re long term’: Miller charts selective growth in Japan, South Korea, and beyond
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Preparing Insurance Data for Agentic AI
item.description: As insurance enters its agentic AI era, data quality is top of mind for CIOs, and for good reason. Deploying agentic AI with confidence requires a different kind of data integration and hygiene strategy than what we saw during the Big Data era. Asking AI agents to handle complex tasks, such as underwriting or claims processing, requires pinpoint data accuracy.

item.title: Trump Set to Sign AI Cybersecurity Directive as Soon as Thursday
item.description: President Donald Trump is poised to issue an executive order as soon as Thursday aimed at bolstering artificial intelligence cybersecurity and has asked tech industry leaders to join for the event, according to people familiar with the matter. The order that Trump is expected to sign would revamp existing cybersecurity information-sharing programs to include AI companies while stopping short of mandatory federal approval of cutting-edge models, Bloomberg News has reported. The directive is set to be signed about a month after Anthropic revealed that its breakthrough Mythos model was extraordinarily adept at finding network vulnerabilities and could pose a major cybersecurity risk.

item.title: Spotify Strikes Deal With Universal Music to Let Premium Users Create AI Covers, Remixes
item.description: Spotify has struck a deal with Universal Music Group to let subscribers create AI-generated covers and remixes of songs, marking the first time the audio-streaming giant will allow users to produce AI content using its platform. Universal Music is home to artists including Taylor Swift, Ariana Grande, Drake and Billie Eilish. Related: BMG Sues Anthropic for Using Bruno Mars, Rolling Stones Lyrics in AI Training The move puts Spotify in more direct competition with startups such as Udio and Suno that allow AI-powered music creation.

item.title: Meta Settles School Suit Over Social Media, Averting First Trial
item.description: Meta and Google went to trial in a first-of-its-kind personal injury suit over youth social media addiction in January in Los Angeles. Related: Snap, YouTube Settle School-Social Media Suit Ahead of Trial In a statement, plaintiffs’ attorneys Lexi Hazam, Previn Warren, Chris Seeger and Ronald Johnson confirmed they had resolved Breathitt County School District’s claims against Meta, as well as its claims against Snap, TikTok and YouTube. reached an agreement to settle a landmark lawsuit alleging that addiction to Instagram and other top social media platforms upended learning across America and pushed U.S.

item.title: Federal Bipartisan Bill Propose $130 Annual EV Fee to Pay for Road Repairs
item.description: House lawmakers proposed bipartisan legislation that would require owners of electric vehicles to pay a $130 fee for road repairs and $35 for some plug-in hybrid models annually. Most revenue for federally funded road repairs is collected through diesel and gasoline taxes, which EVs do not pay. The BUILD America 250 Act is a bipartisan, five-year surface transportation re-authorization bill that invests in America’s roads, bridges, transit, rail transportation, and highway and motor carrier safety programs.

item.title: High-Powered Dads Are Spending Less Time at Work, More on Childcare
item.description: Between the three-year period ending in 2019 and the three-year period ending in 2024, college-educated dads boosted time spent on housework and childcare by more than four hours per week, while reducing paid work by six hours, according to new research . For many fathers, however, doing more unpaid household work has been directly correlated with less time spent on the job, according to Binder. Three-quarters of the difference came from men cutting their hours, rather than women spending more time at work.

item.title: NY State Construction Deaths Down 25% in 2024: Report
item.description: New York construction deaths fell considerably in 2024, when 55 workers died on job sites across the state, down from the year before, when 74 deaths were recorded, according to the New York Committee for Occupational Safety and Health (NYCOSH) annual construction fatality report. In New York City, 19 construction workers died in 2024, following 30 deaths in 2023, the highest single-year total in the city in the decade covered by the report. Analysis of the OSHA’s 31 New York State-based construction fatality investigations in 2024 found that in New York State, 81% of the construction workers who died were non-union.

item.title: The SpaceX IPO Filing Looks Nothing Like Those Of The Elite Group Of Tech Giants It’s Hoping To Join
item.description: At its target valuation, SpaceX would join a rarified club of just seven U.S. SpaceX filed its public IPO prospectus Wednesday, highlighting many amazing things that it has accomplished. As a public company, SpaceX is reportedly seeking a valuation of around $1.5 trillion or more, per The Wall Street Journal .

item.title: NOAA forecasts 55% chance of below-normal Atlantic hurricane season, as El Nino nears
item.description: US weather agency NOAA anticipates an 82% chance of El Niño between May to July and with the warm phase of the El Niño-Southern Oscillation seen to moderate tropical activity in the Atlantic, the first forecast from NOAA for the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season is for a 55% chance of below-normal activity levels. For the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season, beginning June 1st, NOAA’s National Weather Service forecasts 8 to 14 named tropical storms, with 3 to 6 hurricanes, and between 1 and 3 intense hurricanes of Category 3 strength or greater. NOAA said it sees a a 35% chance of a near-normal season, a 10% chance of an above-normal season, and a 55% chance of a below-normal season.

item.title: State Farm gets $1.5bn of reinsurance with Merna Re Enterprise 2026-1 catastrophe bond
item.description: We’ve learned that State Farm returned to the catastrophe bond market in recent weeks and has now secured a significant $1.5 billion of reinsurance protection through a Merna Re Enterprise Ltd. While details are scarce still, with this $1.5 billion issuance of Merna Re Enterprise Series 2026-1 catastrophe bonds State Farm has secured a significant new source of multi-year and fully-collateralized reinsurance from the capital markets. The Merna Re Enterprise 2026-1 cat bond notes will provide State Farm a $1.5 billion roughly three year source of collateralized aggregate reinsurance protection, running to maturity in early July 2029, we’ve learned from sources.

item.title: American Coastal renews core cat reinsurance programme with $1.918bn coverage
item.description: (ACIC) has renewed its core catastrophe reinsurance programme (Core CAT) for 2026/27, purchasing approximately $1.918 billion of occurrence-based limit in the aggregate, an increase of $241.5 million, or 14.4% from the previous year. Florida-based American Coastal Insurance Corp. For the renewed Core CAT programme, facilitated through its insurance subsidiary AmCoastal, ACIC capitalised on the shifting market dynamics by integrating alternative capital, securing an external quota share, and optimising its layers to build a strong protection against single and multi-event hurricane seasons.

item.title: Shory and OrbitWorks partner to advance satellite insurance and space data
item.description: Shory Group, a fully digital AI-driven Insurtech platform and part of Judan Financial Holding, has collaborated with OrbitWorks, the UAE’s first commercial satellite constellation production and operation company, to advance satellite insurance and space data. It will focus on how OrbitWorks’ satellite capabilities and geospatial data can enhance Shory’s risk modelling and underwriting processes, as well as opportunities to extend insurance coverage to space-based assets. The collaboration will explore the intersection of satellite technology and insurance, reflecting the growing convergence between space-based data and risk management.

item.title: Allstate estimates $870m in cat losses for April
item.description: The Allstate Corporation, a provider of personal property and casualty insurance in the US, has estimated catastrophe losses for the month of April 2026 of $870 million or $687 million, after-tax. Comparably, for March 2026, Allstate estimated catastrophe losses of $925 million, or $731 million after tax, which included 15 wind and hail events; just three of these events were responsible for approximately 80% of the month’s total losses. For April 2025, catastrophe losses were estimated at $594 million or $469 million, after-tax.

item.title: AM Best Revises Outlooks to Stable for Missouri Valley Mutual
item.description: AM Best has revised the outlooks to stable from negative and affirmed the Financial Strength Rating of B+ (Good) and the Long-Term Issuer Credit Rating of “bbb-” (Good) of Missouri Valley Mutual Insurance Company (MVMIC) based in Burke, South Dakota. The revision of the outlooks to stable from negative reflects Missouri Valley’s improved operating performance metrics following multiple corrective actions implemented by management. The Credit Ratings (ratings) reflect MVMIC’s balance sheet strength, which AM Best assesses as adequate, as well as its adequate operating performance, limited business profile and appropriate enterprise risk management (ERM).

item.title: Markets/Coverages: MGA Rokstone Launches Cargo Stock-Only Cover for Inventory Risk
item.description: Rokstone, the international specialty re/insurance MGA, announced the launch of a cargo stock-only program, supported by US$25 million of capacity, to support brokers and their clients with smarter, more flexible coverage and competitive premiums. One of just a handful of MGAs with the appetite, capability, and capacity relationships to deliver stock-only coverage, this new program is designed to meet the realities of today’s property and transit exposures, Rokstone said. Rokstone said the program aims to respond to demand for more tailored inventory solutions.
